signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry + Positioning
signals.text: USD/JPY at 155.93 — reports of a US-Iran agreement to end the war drive a -9.65% collapse in WTI oil, easing inflation fears and supporting a -0.67% drop in US 10Y yields. Despite the risk-on headline, the 300bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage sustains the structural bid. JPY COT LF net short -88,543 (30.6% long) remains a crowded carry trade; however, the bull flattener regime and falling oil prices reduce the immediate pressure for a BoJ hike. Intervention risk remains dormant below 160.00 while VIX stays at 16.7 (normal volatility).

signals.title: NZD/CAD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Risk Appetite
signals.text: NZD/CAD at 0.4388 — NZD outperforms as the G8 leader (+0.670%) following reports of Middle East de-escalation, while CAD lags (-0.498%) due to the oil price shock. NZD/USD hits eight-week highs at 0.5968 despite a 150bp carry disadvantage vs the Fed, signaling a shift toward risk-sensitive flows. NZD COT LF net short -16,833 (23.0% long) vs RBNZ hike bias suggests a short-squeeze is underway as specs are forced to cover into the risk-on rally.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7257 — RBA hike to 4.35% vs Fed 3.75% creates a -60bp AUD carry premium, supporting the 1W +0.78% gain. COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long) shows institutional conviction, while retail sentiment is 90% short. This extreme divergence indicates retail is fading the trend, increasing the risk of a capitulation-led extension higher. Bullish momentum is reinforced by the US-Iran peace headlines favoring high-beta currencies.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Macro + Correlation Break
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1774 — Eurozone Services PMI falls into contraction (47.6 vs 50.2 prior), signaling stagflation risks. Despite poor data, EUR/USD vs EuroStoxx correlation has broken to +0.735 (z=+1.89σ), as the pair tracks the broader equity relief rally following the Iran war headlines rather than domestic fundamentals. The 175bp Fed-ECB carry advantage structurally limits upside unless US yields continue to retreat.
